Title: Dissecting the labdane-related diterpenoid biosynthetic gene clusters in rice reveals directional cross-cluster phytotoxicity

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ent-CPP-derived phytocassane branch; also cross-cluster contribution to momilactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative tailoring of diterpene olefin intermediate(s); in planta primarily associated with phytocassane biosynthesis and, together with CYP76M8, required to prevent accumulation of phytotoxic intermediates linked to c4BGC-dependent momilactone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ent-cassadiene-related diterpene intermediate(s) (paper context); possible overlapping activity on syn-pimaradiene-related intermediate(s) in recombinant settings
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate and helps channel flux; indirectly prevents toxic intermediate buildup affecting momilactone-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss together with CYP76M8 causes lesion mimic/hypersensitive response, H2O2 accumulation, defense gene induction, drought sensitivity, and reduced phytocassanes and momilactones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against fungal blast and bacterial leaf blight pathogens; stress-related protection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves after CuCl2 induction; root exudates for some LRDs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CuCl2 elicitation for metabolite profiling; pathogen infection assays; drought sensitivity observations; lesion phenotype enhanced at 30°C and suppressed at 23°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 single and double knockout; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS; DAB staining; RT-qPCR of defense genes; phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double loss with CYP76M8 causes lesion mimic, reactive oxygen accumulation, drought sensitivity, and altered diterpenoid profile
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid simultaneous disruption of CYP76M7 and CYP76M8 when editing diterpenoid clusters; preserve this function to prevent phytotoxicity and defense-related lesion mimic while manipulating rice diterpenoid composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single cyp76m7 mutant showed no lesion mimic. Discussion states CYP76M7 is more important for phytocassane biosynthesis, whereas CYP76M8 is more important for momilactone biosynthesis despite similar recombinant activities.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76M7 is a c2BGC cytochrome P450 contributing mainly to phytocassane biosynthesis in rice. 2. Single knockout did not cause lesion mimic, but combined loss with CYP76M8 reproduced the Δc2bgc lesion mimic/hypersensitive phenotype. 3. The data support that absence of CYP76M7/8 permits accumulation of c4BGC-dependent phytotoxic intermediate(s), revealing cross-cluster metabolic interdependence.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: momilactone branch associated with c4BGC; also within c2BGC gene content
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative tailoring in momilactone biosynthesis; discussion identifies syn-pimaradien-19-al as a likely relevant substrate in the c4BGC-associated pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: syn-pimaradien-19-al (discussed as likely relevant substrate); syn-pimaradiene-related intermediate(s)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxidized momilactone pathway intermediate(s) leading toward momilactones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate/precursor required for momilactone production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not formally established, but critical for momilactone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single loss significantly reduces momilactone biosynthesis; combined loss with CYP76M7 causes lesion mimic/hypersensitive response and strong reductions in both momilactones and phytocassanes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and stress-related function; momilactones are major rice LRDs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CuCl2 elicitation; lesion phenotype temperature sensitive (30°C vs 23°C); pathogen-related context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout and double knockout; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; lesion phenotype analysis; DAB staining; RT-qPCR of defense markers
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption lowers momilactone levels; combined disruption with CYP76M7 causes lesion mimic and drought sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for altering momilactone output, but should not be simultaneously removed with CYP76M7 because of cross-cluster phytotoxicity and lesion mimic risk
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously demonstrated importance to momilactone biosynthesis is confirmed here. Single cyp76m8 mutant lacked lesion mimic, showing nonredundant biosynthetic role but redundant protection against toxic intermediate accumulation with CYP76M7.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76M8 is a c2BGC P450 strongly associated with momilactone biosynthesis in rice. 2. Deletion of c2BGC reduced momilactones consistently with loss of CYP76M8, while cyp76m7/8 double knockout caused stronger momilactone reduction. 3. Together with CYP76M7, CYP76M8 prevents accumulation of phytotoxic c4BGC-derived intermediate(s) that trigger lesion mimic and defense activation when both are absent.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene cyclase (copalyl diphosphate synthase)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: syn-CPP branch leading to momilactones and oryzalexin S
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to syn-copalyl diphosphate (syn-CPP), supplying precursor for momilactone and oryzalexin S biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for c4BGC-dependent momilactones and oryzalexin S; genetic evidence indicates c4BGC/OsCPS4-dependent intermediates are essential for lesion mimic in Δc2bgc background
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CuCl2 induction; lesion phenotype genetic comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BGC deletion phenotyping and metabolite profiling; inference from cps4 and cps2/cps4 mutant phenotypes discussed in Results/Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss abolishes syn-CPP-derived metabolites and is associated with drought sensitivity in cited related work
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key target to modulate syn-CPP-derived diterpenoids, but edits may alter multiple defense metabolites and interact with c2BGC status
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly edited in this study, but central to interpreting directional cross-cluster phytotoxicity and explicitly used in the authors’ genetic model for lesion mimic.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS4 provides syn-CPP precursor for c4BGC-dependent momilactones and oryzalexin S. 2. Loss of c4BGC abolishes momilactones and oryzalexin S, and the absence of lesion mimic in Δc2/c4bgc and cps4-related backgrounds indicates c4BGC-dependent intermediates are necessary for the Δc2bgc lesion phenotype. 3. Thus OsCPS4-dependent flux underlies the directional cross-cluster phytotoxicity model.
